questions.question: Which CSS pseudo-class should be used for keyboard-only focus indicators?
questions.explanation: :focus-visible applies focus styles only when the browser determines focus should be visible (typically keyboard navigation).

questions.question: What is the minimum focus indicator width recommended by WCAG 2.2?
questions.explanation: WCAG 2.2 Success Criterion 2.4.11 (Focus Appearance) requires a minimum 2px thick focus indicator.

questions.question: Which CSS property is best for focus indicators because it doesn't affect layout?
questions.explanation: outline doesn't affect layout (it's drawn outside the element's box) and respects forced colors mode.

questions.question: What does :focus-within do?
options: Styles the parent when any child is focused
Styles siblings of the focused element
questions.explanation: :focus-within applies to an element when it or any of its descendants have focus.

questions.question: What is wrong with `*:focus { outline: none; }`?
options: It removes focus indicators for all users
It doesn't work in all browsers
questions.explanation: This removes visual focus indicators entirely, breaking keyboard navigation for all users.

questions.question: How can you remove focus rings for mouse users while keeping them for keyboard users?
options: Use JavaScript to detect mouse usage
questions.explanation: :focus-visible naturally only applies when the browser determines focus should be visible, typically during keyboard navigation.

questions.question: What contrast ratio must the focus indicator have against adjacent colors?
questions.explanation: WCAG 2.2 requires focus indicators to have at least 3:1 contrast ratio against adjacent non-focus colors.

questions.question: Which ARIA attribute should NOT have a visible focus indicator removed?
questions.explanation: Elements with tabindex are keyboard-focusable and must have visible focus indicators.

questions.question: What is a 'focus trap'?
options: When focus is locked inside a modal until it closes
questions.explanation: A focus trap keeps keyboard focus within a modal or dialog until it's dismissed, preventing users from tabbing to the background content.

questions.question: What is 'roving tabindex'?
options: A pattern where only one element in a group is tabbable at a time
questions.explanation: Roving tabindex sets only the active/selected item in a group (like tabs or a toolbar) to tabindex='0' and others to '-1'.

questions.question: Which system color is used for focus indicators in forced colors mode?
questions.explanation: In forced colors mode, browsers use the system 'Highlight' color for focus indicators.

questions.question: What does WCAG 2.4.7 require?
questions.explanation: WCAG 2.4.7 'Focus Visible' requires any keyboard-operable interface to have a visible focus indicator.

questions.question: Which element should always be focusable for accessibility?
questions.explanation: Skip-to-content links must be focusable (and visible on focus) so keyboard users can bypass repeated navigation.

questions.question: How should custom dropdown components handle focus?
options: Focus the first item when opened
questions.explanation: When a dropdown opens, focus should move to the first menu item. Arrow keys navigate items, Escape closes and returns focus.

questions.question: What is the default browser focus indicator for most elements?
questions.explanation: Most browsers render a default blue outline (2-3px) as the focus indicator for interactive elements.

questions.question: Which of these is a valid reason to use `tabindex="-1"`?
options: To remove an element from tab order
To make an element programmatically focusable
questions.explanation: tabindex="-1" makes an element programmatically focusable via JavaScript but removes it from the natural tab order.

questions.question: What happens when you use Tab on a page with no focusable elements?
questions.explanation: If no interactive elements exist, Tab won't move focus anywhere, which can trap keyboard users.

questions.question: Which pseudo-class would you use to highlight an entire card when focus is inside it?
questions.explanation: :focus-within on the card element will apply styles when any child (link, button, input) receives focus.

questions.question: Should form inputs have the same focus indicator as buttons?
options: No, inputs should show focus inside the field
questions.explanation: Inputs typically use a combination of border color change and box-shadow inside the input area, while buttons use an outer ring.

questions.question: What keyboard key should close a modal and return focus to the triggering element?
questions.explanation: Escape closes modals, and focus must return to the element that triggered the modal opening.
